name: Duty Cycle Monitor
description: Calculates ON time percentage for digital signals or threshold crossings
author: NISystem

input_tag.description: Digital signal or analog to threshold

threshold.description: Threshold for ON state (for analog inputs)

published_tags.name: py.${config.input_tag}_DutyCycle
published_tags.description: Percentage of time signal is ON

published_tags.name: py.${config.input_tag}_Cycles
published_tags.description: Number of ON/OFF cycles in window

code:
# Duty Cycle Monitor
# Tracks ON time percentage for control signals

import time

input_tag = config.get('input_tag', 'Heater_Enable')
threshold = config.get('threshold', 0.5)
window = config.get('window_seconds', 60)

value = tags.get(input_tag, 0)
now = time.time()

# Determine if ON
is_on = value > threshold

# Initialize state
if 'history' not in state:
    state['history'] = []  # List of (timestamp, is_on) tuples
    state['last_state'] = is_on
    state['cycles'] = 0

# Record state change or periodic sample
state['history'].append((now, is_on))

# Count cycles (OFF->ON transitions)
if is_on and not state['last_state']:
    state['cycles'] += 1
state['last_state'] = is_on

# Remove old entries outside window
cutoff = now - window
state['history'] = [(t, s) for t, s in state['history'] if t >= cutoff]

# Calculate ON time
on_time = 0
hist = state['history']
for i in range(len(hist) - 1):
    t1, s1 = hist[i]
    t2, s2 = hist[i + 1]
    if s1:  # If ON during this interval
        on_time += t2 - t1

# Add current interval if ON
if hist and hist[-1][1]:
    on_time += now - hist[-1][0]

# Calculate duty cycle
actual_window = now - hist[0][0] if hist else window
if actual_window > 0:
    duty_cycle = (on_time / actual_window) * 100
else:
    duty_cycle = 0

publish(f'py.{input_tag}_DutyCycle', duty_cycle)
publish(f'py.{input_tag}_OnTime', on_time)
publish(f'py.{input_tag}_Cycles', state['cycles'])
